Details: Cyanide removal by hydrogen peroxide alone is slow; however, hydrogen peroxide with copper as catalyst is shown to be effective. Cyanide removal efficiency is affected by hydrogen peroxide concentration, contact time, pH, copper concentration and temperature. One study showed enhanced cyanide removal with increasing pH in the range of 3.0 to 10.0 [1359]. However, another study showed lower cyanide removal rates at pH 11 compared to pH 8 with copper-impregnated pumice as catalyst [1358]. Cyanide removal rates were reported generally higher at a temperature of 20 °C compared with at 10 °C [1358].
